Michael Simmrin and his team did an amazing job with helping my fiancée out from the medical center she was in. I called him on a Sunday night and assured me he would work hard on her freedom. Within a 24 hour period, my fiancée was out of the deplorable place she was being unfairly treated at. We are both extremely grateful and thankful to Mr. Simmrin. Thank you, thank you, thank you!!!

I highly recommend Mike Simmrin. Mike is a highly competent attorney. He told up front what to expect, how long he anticipated the case would run, and an estimate of the fees.  He kept his word completely.  He is a man of high integrity. He never once asked for a dime until the case resolved (Dismissed!!!) and his fees were within the range we discussed.  He rocks!

My wife was admitted to a psychiatric hospital on a Thursday Morning on a 5150 hold. From that point on I was nothing but stressed out wandering what was going to happen next. Would the hospital keep her for an extended time, would the hospital give her the wrong medicines and make her worse. Speaking to my wife, She was becoming more depressed each day she was in the hospital. She needed quiet and needed to sleep but during the night she said some of the patients would freak out, scream and cause all kinds of disruptions to the hospital. She said that there were dangerous patients who talked about killing people and wanted to fight other patients. She told me that she was afraid they weren't going to release her after the initial 3 day hold and she was told by one of the nurses that they were going to place her on a 14-30 day hold. Knowing that my wife was not getting the care she needed or deserved in this facility, I started searching for 51-50 lawyers and watched Micheal Simmrin's video and decided to call him. Believe it or not, I was able to speak to Michael at 10:30 on a Saturday night. I don't know when he sleeps or rests since he is available 7 days a week and 24 hours a day. He was able to calm me down a bit and told me not to worry he would get her out. Of course, there is nothing that is guaranteed but I did feel some sense of relief. Any time I had a concern, Micheal would get back to me in a relatively short time. Finally, on Monday, I called my wife's social worker to find out what time her hearing would be on Tuesday to find out if she would be held longer and to my surprise, relief and amazement, she said my wife would be released on Tuesday morning. I called Micheal and let him know she was being release and he told me that he had been hammering the hospital all day long to get her release. Micheal also told me that this particular hospital hated and was afraid of him. If I am ever in the same situation again or if you are dealing with a 5150 issue for the first time, call Micheal. If he's not the best lawyer dealing with 5150, I don't know who would be better. The scary thing about a 5150 is that your loved one loses their freedom to leave the hospital until the hospital decides when your loved one is able to leave. How much is your loved one's freedom worth? Call Micheal and you will get the help you need.

I cannot praise enough the compassion and efficiency I encountered immediately upon my first contact with Michael Simmrin. We reached out to him for help getting a family member discharged from a 5150/5250 hold situation. Under the 5150 hold, our relative was placed in a psychiatric facility that mostly houses homeless individuals and drug addicts, even former inmates, and is in the habit of extending their holds while securing more permanent solutions for them.  They were going to extend the involuntary hold placed on our relative while not having even begun treatment.  Needless to say, that environment was not at all appropriate for our relative. I reached out to Michael and within 24 hours, our relative was discharged to seek outpatient care. Michael and his associate Karen provided timely explanations of the process and expert advice on how to proceed, and were available at all time. I would highly recommend hiring Michael for these types of issues.

A Lifesaver in Every Sense

We are beyond grateful for the incredible service we received from Michael Simmrin. He not only helped save me from jail time and court fees, but he also treated us with genuine compassion and respect throughout the entire process. His legal expertise was clear from the start, and he fought for us like we were family.

What truly sets him apart is his willingness to work with us on an affordable price—something that made a huge difference during a stressful time. He never made us feel like just another case. He listened, he cared, and he delivered results that exceeded our expectations.

If you're looking for a lawyer who is not only brilliant in the courtroom but also kind, fair, and deeply committed to his clients, look no further. We will never forget what he did for our family, and we recommend him wholeheartedly.

Thank you, Michael Simmrin, for everything!

A family member was placed on a 5150 hold and transferred to an inpatient facility—not because she truly needed it, but because the hospital wanted to avoid liability. The new facility seemed motivated by profit, ready to exploit laws that would keep her committed for 14 more days. In a moment of desperation late Friday evening, I called Michael. His office picked up immediately—my first sign they truly put clients first. Within an hour, he was already working on my mom’s release and worked tirelessly and persistently all Friday and Saturday.  He kept constant communication with me and responded to all of my questions and concerns.  I was worried that the facility would not release my mother given their reputation. Incredibly, Michael’s persistence got her discharged in under 24 hours, despite the facility’s total lack of cooperation. He kept me informed and reassured throughout. I can't recommend his office highly enough.  Thank you, Michael - you will be my go-to lawyer!

reviews.text: I reached out to them about an accident injury case and after they gave me the agreement to sign I needed some time to read it over because it had a lot of other fees other than the percentage that they take before you or your medical bills get paid. I was getting a lot of text that said "How are you feeling?" as if the person who was trying to get me to sign actually cared about my injury but it was just them trying to convince me to sign. I told him that I might be going with a different firm just those messages from coming and a week later I had decided to sign with them so I reached back out only to get my texts and calls ignored. Looking back, it was for the best that I did not sign that retainer because they're just there to tell you what you want to hear before you sign, their hidden fees are high and it's not sure that you will get any attention after you sign that agreement which will allow them to make more money on the case than you will receive for your pain and suffering. Also, be careful with their reviews. While they claim to have great ratings, most of those reviews are from their previous practice—which was parking tickets, not personal injury cases.

reviews.text: I got T-boned during a left turn by a 17 year old driving a brand new beamer going 70mph on a surface street.  It threw my 6000lb van into the air and landed on the sidewalk.  When I was in the emergency room waiting, I called The Law Office of Pablo G Pinasco because I saw great reviews for it on Google Maps.  I wanted someone local who would give me personalized attention, not a big chain law firm.  Pablo took the time to talk in-depth with me on the very first call (before I signed with him).  Later, when I got back from the hospital, he drove to my house to address my concerns, explain the full procedure and fill out the paperwork.  I went through the doctors they recommended because like many people, I wasn't sure where to go.  The doctors were fine and treated me with respect and concern.  After a few months of therapy and a minor surgical procedure, I was mostly recovered (I'm not sure anyone fully recovers from this sort of thing.)  His staff was responsive and very nice.  They answered any questions swiftly and completely.  The full settlement came in at around 13 months (I hear it frequently takes around 1 year).  Pablo came over and delivered the check personally himself (I've actually never visited his office LOL).  I was extremely satisfied with my share of the settlement amount.  Pablo Pinasco is a great accident attorney I can wholeheartedly recommend. (He even helped me out on unrelated legal advice).  As long as you have patience and follow through with the recommended procedures, I believe you'll have a settlement you will be pleased with.  These things just take time.

I would not recommend Abramson Labor Group. They took on my case, which involved both workers’ compensation and employment law issues, and I ultimately transferred it to another firm.

It was extremely difficult to speak directly with my attorney. Most calls were routed through what felt like a call center style answering service, and messages often went unanswered. Emails also went without response. At one point, I was days away from missing a workers’ compensation deposition because I had not been notified. I only learned about it after repeatedly following up for an update.

I did receive a solid workers’ compensation settlement, and I appreciate that outcome. However, the overall process was frustrating due to the lack of communication and organization. I did not feel confident having them handle my employment matter and chose to move to another firm, where I was able to communicate directly with my attorney and experienced a much more structured and responsive process.

If communication, clarity, and direct access to your lawyer matter to you, I recommend asking detailed questions about how your case will be managed before signing on. Legal matters are stressful enough. You should feel informed, supported, and confident in the team representing you.
The team at Abramson is top notch.  I was kept informed of the process the whole time.
